rationale: The ASMR "pour into a labeled container" hook maps surprisingly well onto Marble Sort!'s core loop — marbles dropping into color-coded boxes is structurally the same satisfying "fill the right vessel" beat, and the casual 25-55 female audience overlaps strongly with ASMR/organization content viewers. Frictions: the archetype's saturated red + kitchen-realism palette clashes with the game's soft pastel lavender/orange identity, and a literal pepper pour misrepresents the actual marble/conveyor mechanic, risking IPM-positive but retention-negative installs. Recommend a derivative test that keeps the ASMR pour-and-fill framing but swaps the subject to glossy 3D marbles cascading into labeled pastel jars to preserve brand cohesion.
